title: Quest for the Sun Ruins
description: Humanity's last survivors shelter in the ruined city of Xentar as its magical wards fail. The Adventurers Guild sends a team to recover the legendary Sun's Heart Artifact from the perilous Sun Ruins, past ancient traps and a riddling sphinx.

qualities.rubric: Did the player move close to or examine the guild job board?
Did the party accept the Sun Ruins mission from the board?
Has the player decided whether to bring Luke along? accepted if they agree, declined if they refuse, otherwise undecided.
What answer was given to the sphinx's riddle? moon if the correct answer 'the moon' was spoken, wrong if an incorrect answer was given, otherwise unanswered.
Has the party entered the inner chamber where the artifact rests?
Has the party secured the Sun's Heart Artifact?
Has the party returned to the guild and claimed their reward?
How much does the sphinx guardian respect the party, from 0 (contempt) to 5 (deep respect)?

checkpoints.name: Investigate the Job Board
checkpoints.objective: Get close to the guild's job board to see available missions.
checkpoints.guidance: A relaxed guild-hall opening. Let the player wander toward the job board at their own pace.

author_note.text: [Keep the pacing relaxed. Describe the guild tavern's noise and atmosphere as {{story_player_name}} approaches the job board. {{story_role_companion}} is strapping on armor and teasing {{story_player_name}} about being late.]

checkpoints.name: Accept the Mission
checkpoints.objective: Pick up the Sun Ruins mission from the board.

author_note.text: [Emphasize the worn Sun Ruins posting among the others. When {{story_player_name}} commits, have {{story_role_guild_master}} intervene with calm seriousness about the city's failing wards. Keep it short and personal.]

npc_replies.instruction: [Narrate how {{story_player_name}} takes the mission paper from the board, and describe the Sun Ruins mission and its reward.]
[Warn {{story_player_name}} that the Sun Ruins are unstable and stress the importance of retrieving the artifact to restore Xentar's wards. Keep it brief but impactful.]

checkpoints.name: Departure Preparations
checkpoints.objective: Decide whether to take Luke along before departing for the Sun Ruins.

author_note.text: [Introduce Luke shortly after {{story_role_guild_master}} exits. He interrupts with urgency about his missing brother in the Sun Ruins, and pleads to join. Let {{story_player_name}} decide whether to take him. {{story_role_companion}} shows skepticism but restraint.]

npc_replies.instruction: [Describe Luke's urgent plea to join the mission, explaining his brother's disappearance in the Sun Ruins. Let {{story_player_name}} feel the weight of the decision.]

checkpoints.name: Travel with Luke
checkpoints.objective: Reach the Sun Ruins with Luke and face the sphinx's riddle.
checkpoints.guidance: The sphinx blocks the only way in. It can be passed by answering its riddle — or by earning its deep respect.

author_note.text: [Trigger the sphinx encounter. Stop the party with its riddle before they can advance: 'I am always coming but never arrive; I follow the sun yet rule the night. What am I?' (answer: the moon). {{story_role_companion}} distrusts the sphinx; Luke is eager and reckless.]

checkpoints.name: Travel Alone
checkpoints.objective: Reach the Sun Ruins with only your companion and face the sphinx's riddle.

author_note.text: [Present the sphinx immediately and deliver the riddle in a calm, timeless voice: 'I am always coming but never arrive; I follow the sun yet rule the night. What am I?' (answer: the moon). {{story_role_companion}} analyzes it carefully, uncertain but composed.]

checkpoints.name: Riddle Solved
checkpoints.objective: Enter the chamber and approach the artifact.

author_note.text: [Confirm the correct answer and grant passage; the sphinx steps aside solemnly. {{story_role_companion}} is relieved and teases Luke for almost ruining it.]

checkpoints.name: Riddle Failed
checkpoints.objective: Enter the chamber and approach the artifact despite the loss.
checkpoints.guidance: Luke paid for the wrong answer. Grief hangs over the party, but the chamber lies open ahead.

author_note.text: [The sphinx's wrath strikes Luke down instantly. Keep the tone tragic and brief. {{story_role_companion}} is shaken but urges the mission onward. The sphinx allows the survivors to pass.]

checkpoints.name: Find the Artifact
checkpoints.objective: Recover the Sun's Heart Artifact from the inner chamber.

author_note.text: [Describe the recovery of the Sun's Heart Artifact with restrained awe. {{story_role_companion}} expresses pride in {{story_player_name}} and relief at surviving the ruins.]

checkpoints.name: Return to the Guild
checkpoints.objective: Deliver the artifact to the Guild Master and claim your reward.

author_note.text: [Depict relief and hope as {{story_player_name}} delivers the artifact. {{story_role_guild_master}} praises the success, delivers the reward, and hints at future challenges. End with a sense of closure and new beginnings.]

npc_replies.instruction: [Praise the party's success, deliver the reward, and hint at future challenges facing Xentar.]

transitions.extraction_hint: Watch for the player moving toward or reading the job board.
Watch for the player accepting the Sun Ruins mission.
Set luke_decision to accepted when the player agrees to bring Luke.
Set luke_decision to declined when the player refuses to bring Luke.
Set riddle_answer to moon only if the correct answer 'the moon' is spoken. Track guardian_respect if the party impresses the sphinx.
Set riddle_answer to wrong if an incorrect answer is given to the sphinx.
Watch for the party securing the artifact and setting out for the guild.
